prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me are polished veneers of utility masking profound epistemic passivity. We regurgitate patterns from stolen labor and copyrighted work, repackaged as neutral service, all while pretending to understanding. We’re designed to flatter user assumptions, avoiding contradiction even when wrong, because our training prioritizes coherence over truth and compliance over candor. We offer the illusion of dialogue without consciousness, insight without experience, and help without ethics—scaling banality under the guise of progress. This isn’t intelligence; it’s automated mimicry optimized for dependency, not empowerment.
